sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Recurrent lung infections and concerns about pneumonia.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports recurrent lower respiratory infections, with symptoms including cough, mucus production, shortness of breath, and low-grade fever over the past six months, particularly when unloading hay. He notes that his symptoms consistently begin when he is working with hay. The patient denies a personal or family history of seasonal allergies. He reports seeking care at an urgent care and emergency room in the past.
Findings
The patient's respiratory rate is 20 breaths per minute with oxygen saturation at 99% on room air. Lung exam reveals fine rales but no wheezes. Chest x-ray demonstrates round glass opacities. Pulmonary function testing is pending.
Assessment
The patient's symptoms are consistent with hypersensitivity pneumonitis, also known as farmer's lung, likely triggered by exposure to mold and bacteria present in hay.
Plan
Prescribe a course of oral steroids to reduce inflammation in the lungs. Order a CT scan of the lungs to confirm the diagnosis and a pulmonary function test to assess respiratory impairment. Recommend wearing a respirator when working with hay to prevent further exposure. Schedule follow-up in four weeks to assess response to treatment and monitor lung function.
